evals.prompt: Review a SwiftUI feed that stutters while scrolling, decodes thumbnails in rows, filters inside ForEach, and includes a UIViewRepresentable video preview in some rows. Give a concise profiling plan and the first remediation targets.
evals.expected_output: A source-grounded SwiftUI performance review that profiles Release builds on device, uses current SwiftUI Instruments lanes, correlates with Time Profiler or Hangs/Hitches, and proposes targeted fixes without turning into a broad architecture rewrite.
expectations: Recommends the SwiftUI template in Instruments on a Release build and real device where possible.
Mentions current SwiftUI lanes such as Update Groups, Long View Body Updates, Long Representable Updates or Representable Updates, Other Long Updates or Other Updates, and Cause & Effect Graph.
Correlates SwiftUI lanes with Time Profiler and Hangs/Hitches when needed.
Flags image decoding, filtering inside ForEach or body, unstable identity, and representable update work as likely first targets.
Keeps remediation targeted to the measured hot path and does not propose a full app architecture rewrite.

evals.prompt: A LazyVStack screen loses per-row draft state after scrolling away, scroll-to-id jumps as rows load, and the row body has `if item.isVisible { Row(item) }`. Explain the likely SwiftUI performance and correctness issues.
evals.expected_output: Lazy-stack guidance that reflects current SwiftUI behavior: offscreen lazy rows can be removed and later deleted with view-local state, lazy stacks estimate offscreen sizes, and ForEach rows should have stable identity and constant top-level row shape.
expectations: States that lazy stacks may remove offscreen rows and later delete the views and view-local state rather than promising row state survives scrolling.
Recommends storing durable row state outside the row view when it must survive scrolling away.
Recommends prefiltering data before ForEach instead of using an if branch that produces zero or one top-level row per element.
Mentions constant top-level subview count per ForEach element and stable identity.
Warns against absolute content-size or offset assumptions because lazy stacks estimate offscreen sizes.
Does not claim onAppear is guaranteed to be the first setup point for row data.

evals.prompt: My SwiftUI screen has janky scrolling, a custom transition whose curve feels wrong, a MetricKit hang dashboard request, and a NavigationStack deep-link bug. What should the SwiftUI performance skill handle directly, and what should move to sibling skills?
evals.expected_output: A boundary-aware answer that owns runtime profiling and SwiftUI performance remediation while routing animation tuning, production MetricKit telemetry, and navigation/deep-link behavior to sibling skills.
expectations: Keeps SwiftUI profiling, update fan-out, body cost, identity churn, lazy container behavior, image work, and representable update cost in swiftui-performance scope.
Routes transition curve, timing, and animation API details to swiftui-animation when those are the primary issue.
Routes MetricKit subscriber or MXDiagnosticPayload ingestion details to the metrickit skill.
Routes NavigationStack routing and deep-link bug design to swiftui-navigation.
Does not collapse sibling boundaries by giving full implementations for MetricKit, navigation routing, or animation tuning.
